Orthogonal Double ML with instrumental variables (OrthoIV)

Source: R/DMLearner.R
OrthoIVLearner.Rd

Cross-fitted nuisance regressions for IV double ML and a linear CATE \(\theta(X)^\top \phi(X)\). Solves the orthogonal moment (single instrument or first column of \(Z\) used in the estimating equations) \(E[(Y - E[Y|X,W] - \theta(X)(T - E[T|X,W]))(Z - E[Z|X,W])] = 0\) when projection = FALSE; if projection = TRUE, the residual treatment uses \(T - E[T|X,W,Z]\) instead of \(T - E[T|X,W]\). No Python/EconML dependency.

Usage

OrthoIVLearner(
  model_y_xw = "auto",
  model_t_xw = "auto",
  model_t_xwz = "auto",
  model_z_xw = "auto",
  projection = FALSE,
  fit_cate_intercept = TRUE,
  discrete_treatment = FALSE,
  discrete_instrument = FALSE,
  cv = 2L,
  random_state = NULL
)

Arguments

model_y_xw, model_t_xw, model_t_xwz, model_z_xw

nuisance learner per equation; see above

projection

logical; if TRUE use \(T - E[T|X,W,Z]\) in the moment

fit_cate_intercept

logical (default TRUE)

discrete_treatment, discrete_instrument

logical (default FALSE)

cv

integer; cross-fitting folds (default 2)

random_state

integer or NULL

Value

Object of class OrthoIVLearner

Nuisance machine learning (first stage)

Each of model_y_xw, model_t_xw, model_t_xwz, model_z_xw is "auto" (maps to "ranger") or one of "lm", "ranger", "glmnet", "xgb". Continuous \(Y\), \(T\), and \(Z\) use regression-style fits. For binary \(T\) (discrete_treatment = TRUE) or binary \(Z\) (discrete_instrument = TRUE with 0/1 labels), glmnet and xgb use logistic (binomial) objectives; ranger fits a forest to 0/1 labels; lm is a linear probability model. Together this covers linear, random-forest, regularized logistic/elastic-net, and gradient-boosted nuisance models as in EconML/DML IV pipelines.

Treatment and instrument types

Continuous \(T\): discrete_treatment = FALSE (default). Binary \(T\): discrete_treatment = TRUE. Multi-level or multi-arm \(T\): this constructor uses a scalar \(T\) vector; encode categories as integers or analyze arms via DoubleML (e.g. DoubleMLPLR with dummy \(D\)) or multiple runs. Continuous or binary instruments: set discrete_instrument when \(Z\) is binary 0/1.

Prediction and evaluation

The predict method returns CATE. To score nuisance predictions (cross-fitted \(\hat{E}[Y|X,W]\), \(\hat{E}[T|\cdot]\), \(\hat{E}[Z|X,W]\) against observed \(Y\), \(T\), \(Z\)), use mlr3measures, e.g. rmse and mae for continuous targets and acc (or custom measures) for binary nuisances.

References

Chernozhukov et al. (2018) Double/debiased ML; EconML IV DML classes; Bach et al. (2021) DoubleML https://docs.doubleml.org

See also

RCausalML-package

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Basic usage
# OrthoIVLearner(...)
} # }
